name: Fleurance and Lavardens Loop
name_original: La randonnée des étoiles – boucle de Fleurance
description: Road cycling The Stars Hike – Fleurance Loop is a moderate 46.2-mile (74.4 km) route with 2624 feet (800 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fleurance traverse a picturesque countryside characterized by rolling hills and open fields. The region, nestled in southwestern France, offers varied terrain with well-maintained surfaces suitable for road bikes. Cyclists can find routes with diverse elevation gains, ranging from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents. The landscape features small farms and ranches, providing a pleasant backdrop for rides. Best road cycling routes around Fleurance The most popular road cycling route is Fleurance and Lavardens Loop, a 46.2 miles (74.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through picturesque countryside scenes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Central Square of Fleurance – Mauvezin loop from Fleurance, a moderate 61.5 miles (99.0 km) path. This route features rolling hills and open fields characteristic of the region. Local road cyclists also love the Saint-Clar Village Center – Saint-Clar loop from Fleurance, a 48.6 miles (78.3 km) trail leading through charming local areas, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Fleurance is defined by picturesque countryside, rolling hills, and small farms and ranches. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels. The routes in Fleurance are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fleurance's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Fleurance?
A: Fleurance offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options from easy rides to moderate and more challenging ascents. The region is known for its picturesque countryside, rolling hills, and well-maintained surfaces, making for a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Fleurance area?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes available around Fleurance, with 11 easy, 75 moderate, and 17 difficult options to choose from.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on road cycling routes around Fleurance?
A: The terrain around Fleurance is characterized by picturesque countryside, rolling hills, and open fields. You'll cycle past small farms and ranches, with routes featuring varied elevation gains from gentle climbs to more demanding ascents. The surfaces are generally well-paved, suitable for road bikes.

Q: Are there any loop trails for road cycling in Fleurance?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Fleurance are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275650677/fleurance-and-lavardens-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fleurance and Lavardens Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24841320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Central Square of Fleurance – Mauvezin loop from Fleurance&lt;/a&gt; also offers a great circular ride.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: While cycling around Fleurance, you'll encounter beautiful countryside scenes, including small farms and ranches. Many routes pass by charming local areas and offer opportunities to visit historical sites. Notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar Village Center&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: While many routes in Fleurance are moderate, there are 11 easy routes that could be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These typically feature shorter distances and less elevation gain, allowing for a more relaxed experience through the scenic countryside.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Fleurance?
A: The road cycling routes in Fleurance are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque countryside, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: Are there options for beginner road cyclists in Fleurance?
A: Absolutely. Fleurance offers 11 easy road cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed pace. These routes typically cover shorter distances and have minimal elevation gain, allowing you to enjoy the beautiful scenery without excessive effort.

Q: What about more challenging routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Fleurance has 17 difficult road cycling routes. These routes often feature longer distances and more significant elevation gains, providing a rigorous workout while still showcasing the region's stunning landscapes. An example of a moderate route with notable elevation is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275650677/fleurance-and-lavardens-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fleurance and Lavardens Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 46.2 miles with 2624 feet of elevation.

Q: Are there scenic road cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: Yes, the entire region around Fleurance is known for its scenic beauty. Routes wind through 'beautiful countryside scenes,' including small farms and ranches, and offer views of rolling hills and open fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36644462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunflower Fields – Central Square of Fleurance loop from Mourret&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that highlights the region's natural charm.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails near Fleurance?
A: While specific parking locations aren't detailed for each route, Fleurance itself and its surrounding villages offer various public parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or use navigation apps to locate convenient parking near your chosen starting point, such as the Central Square of Fleurance.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fleurance?
A: The region's climate generally allows for pleasant cycling during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons typically offer favorable weather conditions for enjoying the open roads and scenic countryside. Winter cycling is also possible, but conditions may vary, so checking the local weather forecast is always recommended.
tips: The bastide of Fleurance was founded in the 13th century. The central arcaded square hosts a beautiful 19th century hall. At the end of July, the Cuivro foliz, a festival of street brass bands, takes place there.
Slightly out of the village we find the Château de Latour. The eastern part of this facade was redesigned in the 19th century. The castle is surmounted by a 19th century keep.

Nice view of the village.
In the old town you will find a few cafes and restaurants.

The Castle, built in the 17th century. has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1961.

faqs.question: Is The Stars Hike – Fleurance Loop suitable for road cycling, despite its name?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! While the name includes 'Hike,' this specific komoot route, 'The Stars Hike – Fleurance Loop,' is expertly designed for racebikes. It covers approximately 74.4 km with a moderate difficulty, making it a great option for cyclists looking for a scenic ride around Fleurance.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. With an elevation gain of about 800 meters over 74.4 km, it offers a good challenge without being overly strenuous, suitable for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting points along the way. Highlights include the Central Square of Fleurance, the area around Miramont-Latour, and the historic Lavardens Castle. These offer great opportunities for a short break or to explore local history.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and are there parking options?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop, so you can technically start at any point. A common and convenient starting point would be near the Central Square of Fleurance, where you can often find public parking. Specific parking details might vary, so checking local signage upon arrival is always a good idea.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring or autumn months. The weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check the local forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You might find yourself on parts of the Cœur de Gascogne, Circuit VTT n°12, Circuit VTT n°19, Circuit VTT n°16, and Circuit VTT n°11, among others. These intersections are generally brief as the route primarily follows its own path.

faqs.question: Is The Stars Hike – Fleurance Loop dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, bringing dogs along would typically require them to be in a trailer or basket designed for cycling. The route itself doesn't have specific dog-friendly amenities like water stations for pets, so plan accordingly if you bring your furry friend.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths around Fleurance. However, always be respectful of private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a racebike route, you can expect mostly paved roads. There will be ascents and descents contributing to the 800 meters of elevation gain, but the surfaces should be suitable for road bike tires. Be prepared for some rolling hills characteristic of the region.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or rest stops along the way?
faqs.answer: Fleurance itself offers various amenities, including cafes and shops, especially near the Central Square of Fleurance. As you venture out, smaller villages like Miramont-Latour might have limited options. It's always wise to carry enough water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: For an average cyclist, the route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the weather conditions.
